title: QA Counterfactual Descent FAIL — documents the non-minimal-path failure mode that CFD_MINIMAL is designed to catch
_fixture_purpose: Documented FAIL fixture. Demonstrates the failure mode that CFD_MINIMAL is designed to catch: a fixture declares a counterfactual path that is legal AND flips the spec but is LONGER than the BFS-recomputed shortest path. The body fields below intentionally encode such a non-minimal path so a reviewer can see exactly what the CFD_MINIMAL gate would have flagged. The declared 3-step path qa_step→qa_step→scalar_mult_3 from (1,1) reaches a satellite state but is non-minimal: the 1-step scalar_mult_3 from (1,1) → (3,3) is the BFS-recomputed shortest path.

counterfactual_test_cases.name: nonminimal_3_step_path_from_(1,1)
counterfactual_test_cases.comment: Deliberately non-minimal: this 3-step path reaches satellite (6,9) but the BFS-recomputed shortest from (1,1) is the 1-step scalar_mult_3 path to (3,3). All steps are individually legal, all successor states are correct, and the terminal flips PASS — but path length 3 > 1 = BFS-shortest, so CFD_MINIMAL gate would catch it.

fail_ledger.message: Deliberately wrong: declared 3-step path is legal (all generators in declared_generators, all successors correct) and flips PASS at the terminal state, but is non-minimal — the BFS-recomputed shortest path from (1,1) under {qa_step, scalar_mult_2, scalar_mult_3} with declared_pass_classes=['cosmos'] is the 1-step scalar_mult_3 path to (3,3). If result were not FAIL, the validator would emit: 'CFD_MINIMAL[0]: declared path length=3 != BFS-shortest length=1; declared path is non-minimal'.
